volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 2.222 - Section 301)

sections.label: Section 2.222
sections.original: οὐ θέμις , ὦ ξέναι .
sections.translation: Nay , ye must not , stranger ladies .

sections.label: Section 2.222a
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἂν
sections.translation: May I ask thee about something I have heard ?

sections.label: Section 2.223a
sections.original: τίνα τήνδε θέλεις ;
sections.translation: What wouldst thou ask ?

sections.label: Section 2.223b
sections.original: ἆρʼ ὄντως μέσον ὀμφαλὸν
sections.translation: Is it really true that the temple of Phoebus stands upon the centre of the world ?

sections.label: Section 2.224a
sections.original: στέμμασί γʼ ἐνδυτόν , ἀμφὶ δὲ Γοργόνες .
sections.translation: Aye , there it stands with garlands decked and gorgeous all around .

sections.label: Section 2.225
sections.original: οὕτω καὶ φάτις αὐδᾷ .
sections.translation: E ' en so the legend saith .

sections.label: Section 2.226
sections.original: εἰ μὲν ἐθύσατε πέλανον πρὸ δόμων
sections.translation: If ye have offered a sacrificial cake before the shrine and have aught ye wish to ask Phoebus , approach the altar ; but enter not the inmost sanctuary , save ye have sacrificed sheep .

sections.label: Section 2.230
sections.original: ἔχω μαθοῦσα · θεοῦ δὲ νόμον
sections.translation: I understand ; but we have ho mind to trespass against the god ’ s law ; the pictures here without will amuse us .

sections.label: Section 2.233
sections.original: πάντα θεᾶσθʼ , ὅ τι καὶ θέμις , ὄμμασι .
sections.translation: Feast your eyes on all ye may .

sections.label: Section 2.233a
sections.original: μεθεῖσαν δεσπόται
sections.translation: My mistress gave me leave to see these vaulted chambers .

sections.label: Section 2.234a
sections.original: δμῳαὶ δὲ τίνων κλῄζεσθε δόμων ;
sections.translation: Whose handmaids do ye avow yourselves ?

sections.label: Section 2.235
sections.original: Παλλάδι σύνοικα τρόφιμα μέλα-
sections.translation: The temple , where Pallas dwells , is the nursing-home of my lords . But lo ! here is she of whom thou askest .

sections.label: Section 237
sections.original: γενναιότης σοι , καὶ τρόπων τεκμήριον
sections.translation: Lady , whosoe ’ er thou art , I see thou art of noble birth , and thy bearing proves thy gentle breeding . For from his bearing one may mostly judge

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: τὸ σχῆμʼ ἰδών τις εἰ πέφυκεν εὐγενής .
sections.translation: whether a man is nobly born . Yet am I much amazed to see thee close thine eyes in grief and with tears bedew thy noble face , when thou standest face to face with the holy oracle of Loxias . Why , lady , art thou thus disquieted ?

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ὃ πάντες ἄλλοι γύαλα λεύσσοντες θεοῦ
sections.translation: Here , where all others show their joy at sight of Phoebus ’ sanctuary , thine eye is wet with tears .

sections.label: Section 247
sections.original: ὦ ξένε , τὸ μὲν σὸν οὐκ ἀπαιδεύτως ἔχει
sections.translation: Most courteously , sir stranger , dost thou express surprise at these my tears ; the sight of this temple of Apollo

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: μνήμην παλαιὰν ἀνεμετρησάμην τινά ·
sections.translation: recalled to me a memory of long ago , and somehow my thoughts went wandering home , though I am here myself . Ah , hapless race of women ! ah , ye reckless gods ! What shall I say ? to what standard shall we refer justice if through the injustice of our lords and masters we are brought to ruin ?

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: τί χρῆμʼ ἀνερμήνευτα δυσθυμῇ , γύναι ;
sections.translation: Why , lady , art thou thus cast down , past all finding out ?

sections.label: Section 256
sections.original: οὐδέν · μεθῆκα τόξα · τἀπὶ τῷδε δὲ
sections.translation: ’ Tis naught ; I have shot my bolt ; for what remains , I say no more , nor seek thou further to inquire .

sections.label: Section 258
sections.original: τίς δʼ εἶ ; πόθεν γῆς ἦλθες ; ἐκ ποίας πάτρας
sections.translation: Who art thou and whence ? who is the father that begat thee ? by what name are we to call thee ?

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: Κρέουσα μέν μοι τοὔνομʼ , ἐκ δʼ Ἐρεχθέως
sections.translation: Creusa is my name , the daughter of Erechtheus I ; my native land is Athens .

sections.label: Section 262
sections.original: ὦ κλεινὸν οἰκοῦσʼ ἄστυ γενναίων τʼ ἄπο
sections.translation: A glorious city thine , lady , a noble line of ancestry ! with what reverence I behold thee !

sections.label: Section 264
sections.original: τοσαῦτα κεὐτυχοῦμεν , ὦ ξένʼ , οὐ πέρα .
sections.translation: Thus far , no further goes my luck , good sir .

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν ἀληθῶς , ὡς μεμύθευται βροτοῖς ⋯ ;
sections.translation: Pray , is the current legend true —

sections.label: Section 266
sections.original: τί χρῆμʼ ἐρωτᾷς , ὦ ξένʼ , ἐκμαθεῖν θέλων ;
sections.translation: What is thy question ? I fain would learn .

sections.label: Section 267
sections.original: ἐκ γῆς πατρός σου πρόγονος ἔβλαστεν πατήρ ;
sections.translation: Was thy father ’ s grandsire really sprung from Earth ?

sections.label: Section 268
sections.original: Ἐριχθόνιός γε · τὸ δὲ γένος μʼ οὐκ ὠφελεῖ .
sections.translation: Yes , Erichthonius was ; but my high birth avails me not

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: ἦ καί σφʼ Ἀθάνα γῆθεν ἐξανείλετο ;
sections.translation: Is it true Athena reared him from the ground ?

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: ἐς παρθένους γε χεῖρας , οὐ τεκοῦσά νιν .
sections.translation: Aye , and into maidens ’ hands , though not his mother ’ s —

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: δίδωσι δʼ , ὥσπερ ἐν γραφῇ νομίζεται ⋯ ;
sections.translation: Consigned him , did she ? as ’ tis wont to be set forth in painting .

sections.label: Section 272
sections.original: Κέκροπός γε σῴζειν παισὶν οὐχ ὁρώμενον .
sections.translation: Yes , to the daughters of Cecrops , to keep him safe unseen .

sections.label: Section 273
sections.original: ἤκουσα λῦσαι παρθένους τεῦχος θεᾶς .
sections.translation: I have heard the maidens opened the ark wherein the goddess laid him .

sections.label: Section 274
sections.original: τοιγὰρ θανοῦσαι σκόπελον ᾕμαξαν πέτρας .
sections.translation: And so they died , dabbling with their blood the rocky cliff .

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: εἶἑν ·
sections.translation: Even so . But what of this next story ? Is it true or groundless ?

sections.label: Section 276
sections.original: τί χρῆμʼ ἐρωτᾷς ; καὶ γὰρ οὐ κάμνω σχολῇ .
sections.translation: What is thy question ? Ask on , I have no calls upon my leisure .

sections.label: Section 277
sections.original: πατὴρ Ἐρεχθεὺς σὰς ἔθυσε συγγόνους ;
sections.translation: Did thy sire Erechtheus offer thy sisters as a sacrifice ?

sections.label: Section 278
sections.original: ἔτλη πρὸ γαίας σφάγια παρθένους κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: For his country ’ s sake he did endure to slay the maids as victims .

sections.label: Section 279
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐξεσώθης πῶς κασιγνήτων μόνη ;
sections.translation: And how didst thou , alone of al thy sisters , escape ?

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: βρέφος νεογνὸν μητρὸς ἦν ἐν ἀγκάλαις .
sections.translation: I was still a tender babe in my mother ’ s arms .

sections.label: Section 281
sections.original: πατέρα δʼ ἀληθῶς χάσμα σὸν κρύπτει χθονός ;
sections.translation: Did the earth really open its mouth and swallow thy father ?

sections.label: Section 282
sections.original: πληγαὶ τριαίνης ποντίου σφʼ ἀπώλεσαν .
sections.translation: The sea-god smote and slew him with his trident .

sections.label: Section 283
sections.original: Μακραὶ δὲ χῶρός ἐστʼ ἐκεῖ κεκλημένος ;
sections.translation: Is there a spot there called Macrae ?

sections.label: Section 284
sections.original: τί δʼ ἱστορεῖς τόδʼ ; ὥς μʼ ἀνέμνησάς τινος .
sections.translation: Why ask that ? what memories thou recallest !

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: τιμᾷ σφε Πύθιος ἀστραπαί τε Πύθιαι .
sections.translation: Doth the Pythian god with his flashing fire do honour to the place ?

sections.label: Section 286
sections.original: τιμᾶ τιμᾶ · ὡς μήποτʼ ὤφελόν σφʼ ἰδεῖν .
sections.translation: Honour , yes ! Honour , indeed ! would I had never seen the spot !

sections.label: Section 287
sections.original: τί δὲ στυγεῖς σὺ τοῦ θεοῦ τὰ φίλτατα ;
sections.translation: How now ? dost thou abhor that which the god holds dear ?

sections.label: Section 288
sections.original: οὐδέν · ξύνοιδʼ ἄντροισιν αἰσχύνην τινά .
sections.translation: No , no ; but I and that cave are witnesses of a deed of shame .

sections.label: Section 289
sections.original: πόσις δὲ τίς σʼ ἔγημʼ Ἀθηναίων , γύναι ;
sections.translation: Lady , who is the Athenian lord that calls thee wife ?

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: οὐκ ἀστός , ἀλλʼ ἐπακτὸς ἐξ ἄλλης χθονός .
sections.translation: No citizen of Athens , but a stranger from another land .

sections.label: Section 291
sections.original: τίς ; εὐγενῆ νιν δεῖ πεφυκέναι τινά .
sections.translation: Who is he ? he must have been one of noble birth .

sections.label: Section 292
sections.original: Ξοῦθος , πεφυκὼς Αἰόλου Διός τʼ ἄπο .
sections.translation: Xuthus , son of Aeolus , sprung from Zeus .

sections.label: Section 293
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ξένος σʼ ὢν ἔσχεν οὖσαν ἐγγενῆ ;
sections.translation: And how did he , a stranger , win thee a native born ?

sections.label: Section 294
sections.original: Εὔβοιʼ Ἀθήναις ἔστι τις γείτων πόλις ⋯
sections.translation: Hard by Athens lies a neighbouring township , Euboea .

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: ὅροις ὑγροῖσιν , ὡς λέγουσʼ , ὡρισμένη .
sections.translation: With a bounding line of waters in between , so I have heard .

sections.label: Section 296
sections.original: ταύτην ἔπερσε Κεκροπίδαις κοινῷ δορί .
sections.translation: This did he sack , making common cause with Cecrops ’ sons .

sections.label: Section 297
sections.original: ἐπίκουρος ἐλθών ; κᾆτα σὸν γαμεῖ λέχος ;
sections.translation: Coming as an ally , maybe ; he won thy hand for this ?

sections.label: Section 298
sections.original: φερνάς γε πολέμου καὶ δορὸς λαβὼν γέρας .
sections.translation: Yes , this was his dower of battle , the prize of his prowess .

sections.label: Section 299
sections.original: σὺν ἀνδρὶ δʼ ἥκεις ἢ μόνη χρηστήρια ;
sections.translation: Art thou come to the oracle alone , or with thy lord ?

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: σὺν ἀνδρί . σηκοὺς δʼ ἐκστρέφει Τροφωνίου .
sections.translation: With him . But he is now visiting the cavern of Trophonius .

sections.label: Section 301
sections.original: πότερα θεατὴς ἢ χάριν μαντευμάτων ;
sections.translation: As a spectator merely , or to consult the oracle ?
